confidence_note: Manifest is the primary Android app contract surface.
title: Android manifest foundation
summary: The manifest declares package identity, components, permissions, exported state, SDK targets, and other system-facing metadata.

confidence_note: Network and trust settings are key Android metadata concepts.
title: Network and signing basics
summary: Network security config, signing certificate lineage, and SDK/API level shape trust and compatibility context for Android apps.

confidence_note: Android activities are the primary UI entry components.
title: Activity basics
summary: Activities are user-facing components declared in the manifest and often connected to launcher or deep-link intent filters.

title: Service basics
summary: Services run background tasks and can become suspicious when combined with persistence, hidden startup, or abusive accessibility behavior.

confidence_note: Broadcast receivers often express event-driven behavior.
title: Broadcast receiver basics
summary: Receivers respond to system or app broadcasts such as BOOT_COMPLETED and can indicate persistence or event-triggered behavior.

confidence_note: Content providers expose structured app data surfaces.
title: Content provider basics
summary: Providers expose structured app data and can create privacy risk when exported or weakly protected.

confidence_note: Intent filters are central to component reachability.
title: Intent filter basics
summary: Intent filters describe how activities, services, and receivers can be reached by actions, categories, and data URIs.

title: Exported component basics
summary: Exported components can be reachable from outside the app and should be interpreted with intent filters, permissions, and declared role.

confidence_note: Package identity and SDK targeting anchor app context.
title: Package identity and SDK basics
summary: Package name, signing lineage, minSdkVersion, and targetSdkVersion provide identity and compatibility context for Android app analysis.
